data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: OGN, SLP, TGLS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched up Friday as March core retail inflation came in slightly cooler than expected, though the U.S.-Iran ceasefire relief rally faded amid doubts over its durability, regional friction, and minimal improvement in Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic, reintroducing a risk premium, and pushing oil back toward the $100 mark.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Organon (OGN) +16% – Shares jumped after media reports from India indicated that Mumbai-based generic drugmaker Sun Pharmaceutical Industries had submitted a ~$12B binding bid for the company following months of due diligence, with financing support from JPMorgan Chase & Co. and MUFG. The potential deal, if completed, would mark the largest overseas acquisition by an Indian pharma firm, while Sun Pharma’s shares declined in Mumbai as investors weighed execution and financing risks.
  • Simulations Plus (SLP) +12% - Shares surged after a Q2 beat, with non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 topping estimates and revenue rising 8.3% Y/Y to $24.3M, also ahead of expectations. While FY2026 revenue guidance of $79M–$82M remains broadly in line with consensus and other operating metrics were unchanged, the company lowered its adjusted EPS outlook to $0.75–$0.85 (from $1.03–$1.10) due to a higher expected tax rate. Investors appeared encouraged by solid execution and margin targets of 26%–30% EBITDA alongside a strong software revenue mix outlook.
  • CoreWeave (CRWV) +4% – Shares advanced after securing a deal with Anthropic to provide computing capacity for its Claude AI models, reinforcing strong demand for AI infrastructure. The agreement includes a phased rollout with potential expansion, adding to recent momentum following a massive $21B cloud capacity deal with Meta Platforms, as hyperscalers and AI firms accelerate spending to support next-generation workloads.
  • Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) +3% – Shares rose after reporting strong March 2026 revenue of NT$415.19B, up 45.2% Y/Y and 30.7% sequentially, driven by robust demand for AI-related chips; Q1 revenue totaled NT$1.13T, slightly ahead of consensus and up 35.1% Y/Y, reinforcing momentum in advanced-node demand. Investors now focused on the company’s full Q1 earnings report scheduled for April 16.

Biggest stock losers

  • Tecnoglass (TGLS) -7% - Shares fell after the company updated its FY2026 outlook to reflect the impact of a newly implemented 10% U.S. tariff on aluminum window imports, now guiding for adjusted EBITDA of $225M–$245M, implying a ~$50M hit vs. the prior midpoint. While management outlined mitigation steps, including pricing actions starting with May orders, logistics optimization, increased automation, and headcount rationalization, it expects only a partial offset in 2026, with full tariff neutralization targeted by 2027.
  • Ares Management (ARES) -3% – Shares declined after reports that it is sounding out investors for a new ~$20B direct lending fund, even as rising redemption pressure weighs on sentiment across private credit. The firm recently capped withdrawals from its Ares Strategic Income Fund after receiving $1.2B in Q1 redemption requests, part of a broader ~$20B+ pullback from the asset class impacting peers like Apollo Global Management (APO), Ares (ARES), Blackstone (BX), Blue Owl Capital (OWL), and KKR (KKR), signaling growing investor caution despite continued fundraising efforts.

data.title: Visionary Holdings receives Nasdaq minimum bid price notification
data.description:
• Visionary Holdings (GV) on Friday said it has received a notification letter from Nasdaq indicating that it is not in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement set forth in listing rules for continued listing.
  • According to the notification letter, the closing bid price of the company’s common shares was below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days, from February 23, 2026, to April 6, 2026.
  • The notification has no immediate effect on the listing of the company’s common shares, which will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “GV.”
  • GV is +2.07% to $0.31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Large Cap industrial stocks ranked by quant ratings before earnings season
data.description:
The State Street Industrial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLI) surged 4.3% in the first quarter of 2026, against the broader S&P 500 Index, which fell 4.6% over the same period.

As companies prepare to report their results for the quarter gone by, Seeking Alpha provides a snapshot of how companies rank across key factors such as valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and estimate revisions.

Below is an overview of small-cap financial stocks with market capitalizations above $10B, highlighting those with the highest and lowest quant ratings ahead of Q1 2026 results.

Top quant-rated companies, with a market capitalization above $10B:

Modine Manufacturing (MOD), Quant rating: Strong Buy 4.95.

FedEx (FDX), Quant rating: Strong Buy 4.91.

ATI (ATI), Quant rating: Strong Buy 4.89.

Swire Pacific (SWRAY), Quant rating: Strong Buy 4.85.

Deutsche Post AG (DHLGY), Quant rating: Strong Buy 4.83.

Bottom quant-rated companies, with a market capitalization above $10B:

DiDi Global (DIDIY), Quant rating: Sell 1.82.

Copart (CPRT), Quant rating: Sell 1.79.

Rheinmetall AG (RNMBY), Quant rating: Sell 1.69.

MTU Aero Engines AG (MTUAY), Quant rating: Sell 1.62.

Thomson Reuters (TRI), Quant rating: Strong Sell 1.42.

data.title: Trident Digital Partners with Ripple to launch RLUSD Blockchain Payments in Ghana
data.description:
• Trident Digital Tech (TDTH) signed a strategic agreement with Ripple Strategy to deploy RLUSD stablecoin infrastructure.
  • The partnership will support TDTH’s Africa initiative focused on blockchain-based payment and tax systems in Ghana.
  • TDTH plans to onboard about 2.1M MSMEs onto a unified blockchain-powered digital platform.
  • The system will enable automated tax calculation, collection, and direct remittance to the government.
  • Ripple USD (RLUSD) will power a crypto-enabled settlement layer targeting underbanked and informal sectors.
  • The platform is expected to improve MSME access to credit, reduce transaction costs, and accelerate financial inclusion.
  • Pilot rollouts across African markets are targeted for mid-2026, pending regulatory approvals.
  • TDTH shares down 5% premarket.

data.title: Small-cap consumer staples stocks with A grade EPS revisions
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season is set to begin, the consumer staples sector is drawing attention amid ongoing tariff-driven cost pressures, shifting consumer spending patterns, and persistent uncertainty in the broader macro environment. 

Within the small-cap space, where companies tend to have more concentrated exposure to specific product categories and consumer demographics, upward EPS revisions carry particular weight as a signal of improving fundamental momentum.

The following list highlights small-cap consumer staples stocks that have earned a Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A or higher heading into earnings season, spanning personal care products, packaged foods, and food retail

The list includes Herbalife Ltd. (HLF), a personal care products company with a market cap of $1.57B and a Strong Buy quant rating of 4.81. B&G Foods, Inc. (BGS) and The Honest Company, Inc. (HNST) follow, representing packaged foods and personal care products respectively. Natural Grocers by Vitamin Cottage, Inc. (NGVC) rounds out the list as a food retailer with a market cap of $612.68M.

Here is the list:

  1. Herbalife Ltd. (HLF), EPS revision: A+

  2. B&G Foods, Inc. (BGS), EPS revision: A

  3. The Honest Company, Inc. (HNST), EPS revision: A+

  4. Natural Grocers by Vitamin Cottage, Inc. (NGVC), EPS revision: A+

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS)

data.title: Treasury yields steady as softer core CPI reinforces bets on single Fed cut in 2026
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields were largely unchanged after the latest inflation report suggested underlying price pressures were not as strong as feared, even as rising energy costs linked to the Iran conflict pushed headline risks higher.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) hovered around 4.30%, reflecting stable long-term expectations. On the short end, the 2-year yield (US2Y) stood near 3.78%, showing that markets see little immediate change in the Federal Reserve’s policy stance. The 30-year yield ticked slightly higher, rising about one basis point to 4.90%.

Overall, the limited movement in yields indicates that investors took the data in stride. The softer core inflation reading offered some reassurance that price pressures are not intensifying, helping offset concerns stemming from higher energy prices.

At the same time, rate markets are increasingly aligned around a modest easing path. Traders are now pricing in just one Federal Reserve rate cut in 2026, reflecting a view that while inflation is easing gradually, it remains persistent enough to keep policymakers cautious.

data.title: VinFast doubles domestic EV deliveries in March
data.description:
VinFast Auto (VFS) reported a 127% increase in domestic deliveries for March to 27,609 electric vehicles (EVs) in Vietnam for March. For the first quarter, total deliveries reached 53,684 EVs to reinforce VinFast's (VFS) position as a leader in its home market. 

The company’s best-selling model in March was the Limo Green, with 6,795 units delivered, followed by the VF 3 (4,729 units) and a combined 4,218 units of the VF 5 and its derivative, the Herio Green. Other notable models included the VF 6 (3,152 units), VF MPV 7 (2,521 units), Minio Green (1,969 units), VF 7 (1,732 units), and the EC Van (1,136 units), targeted for transportation services. 

Over the first quarter, the Limo Green (12,471 units) and VF 3 (10,188 units) remained the top sellers, while the VF 5 and Herio Green together accounted for 8,672 deliveries. Additional shipments in the quarter included 6,679 VF 6 vehicles, 3,809 Minio Green vehicles, 3,686 VF MPV 7 vehicles, and 3,514 VF 7 vehicles—reflecting continued traction across VinFast's (VFS) expanding EV lineup.

data.title: Braiin approves 3-for-1 stock split; shares up
data.description:
• Braiin Limited (BRAI) announced approval of a 3-for-1 forward stock split by its board and shareholders.
  • The split aims to lower the share price to make it more accessible for retail investors.
  • Shareholders on record as of April 27, 2026 will receive two additional shares per share held.
  • Additional shares will be distributed after market close on April 28, 2026.
  • Trading on a split-adjusted basis is expected to begin on April 29, 2026.
  • BRAI shares up 6.4% premarket.

data.title: Ares Management plans ~$20B private credit fund - report
data.description:
Ares Management (ARES) is sounding out investors for a direct lending fund with a preliminary total target size of ~$20B, people with knowledge of the matter told Bloomberg News.

In the later part of March, Ares capped redemptions from its Ares Strategic Income Fund at 5% after receiving $1.2B in redemption requests during Q1, representing 11.6% of its $10.7B asset base.

Investors are said to have sought to get back more than $20B of their investments from private credit firms in Q1 2026, a sign of the increasing concern after a boom in the asset class.

The sector, including Apollo Global Management (APO), Ares (ARES), Blackstone (BX), Blue Owl Capital (OWL), and KKR (KKR), received $20.8B in redemption requests during the quarter.

The Ares Senior Direct Lending Fund IV is expected to have a formal launch sometime in the summer. The fund is significantly smaller in terms of target size than its previous record-breaking vehicle of $33.6B, according to the people who asked not to be identified discussing confidential talks.

The new fund targets equity commitments in the range of $10B to $12B, the report noted.

The smaller target size is expected to speed up the timeline to raise and deploy capital, according to the sources, who also said the plans are at an early stage and can change.

The media report follows an announcement that Whitestone REIT (WSR) entered into a definitive merger agreement with Ares Real Estate funds to be acquired for $19/share in an all-cash transaction valued at around $1.7B.

data.title: AMTD Group provides update on $328 million hospitality transactions
data.description:
• AMTD Group (AMTD) and related entities said on Friday they are progressing toward the expected closing of hospitality-related transactions totaling approximately US$328 million within the next two to three weeks.
  • The companies said the transactions include assets in Australia, Malaysia, and the United Kingdom, covering hotels and an office tower across multiple international cities.
  • AMTD said the completion of the deals would mark a milestone in the expansion of its global hospitality portfolio.
  • AMTD +1.01% premarket to $1.0.
  • Source: Press Release
